raw_consumer_statement:
I have filed XXXX complaints with CFPB. The last one was # XXXX XXXX XXXX I have not been able to respond back with this complaint until now. The complaint was that I returned XXXX XXXX televisions back to XXXX, using the pre paid USPS return labels that was included in the boxes. XXXX printed XXXX XXXX numbers on USPS labels which made it impossible for the items to be return back to them. USPS clerk gave me a print out ran on their intranet showing the tvs were picked up from the XXXX, AL post office and delivered at the XXXX mail processing center, but they could complete the shipping process because of the invalid labels. My complaint is that this was done on purpose by XXXX to not receive the items back. Citi sent a letter reference XXXX stating that XXXX sent them a picture of the package being delivered. Again, that fact has never been denied. I also have proof that the items were received by the XXXX, GA mail processing center XXXX days after I

received them.. I have been paying on this because I do not want to ruin my credit. Is it too late to have this investigated once more? I have contacted their corporate office several times. I do not know what else to do. There are XXXX complaints on file better explaining everything. Do I need to re do all of it? yes, the items were delivered, but I returned them using the pre paid labels XXXX provided. Do I need to scan the print out from USPS again showing they received the items in their mail center. The post clerk will verify the items were received in that office to be sent on to the mail processing center, This is XXXX mistake or con. They printed the invalid labels. The consumers should not have to know each carrier tracking number to know that there was a problem with the labels.
